H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence=strong. Largest HEALING sample with strong performance. Qualitative (n=29) shows 12/29 noticeable and 7/23 slight improvement; settle at 10 min (11). Music loved (9) and liked (10), rhythm balanced/calming (10/18). YouTube views=30 highest in HEALING but retention NULL — engagement signal present but unconfirmed. AutoGluon predicts 6.86, marginally below observed.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: One of the most helpful protocols for overstimulation — most people felt better within ten minutes and loved the music, though YouTube reach is still small.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, confidence=strong. Qualitative (n=21) shows 10/21 noticeable improvement, settle 10 min (10). Music liked (10) but neutral for 6. Rhythm split between very settling (7) and balanced (7). YouTube views=5 indicate limited reach despite consistent outcomes.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Reduced overstimulation for many after about ten minutes, with very low YouTube exposure despite consistent benefits.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful. Qualitative (n=23) shows 10/23 noticeable and 4/23 moderate improvement. Distinctive activity: 8/23 used while trying to sleep. Rhythm balanced (10) and very settling (10), music liked (11). AutoGluon prediction (6.86) matches observed closely.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Calmed people struggling with overwhelm, especially those using it for sleep; improvement was common though not universal.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong. Qualitative data (n=23) shows strong improvement reports with 10/23 marking moderate improvement and 8/23 noticeable improvement; settle time clusters at 10 minutes for 9 respondents. Listeners praise rhythm as balanced/calming (13/23) and music as liked/loved (16/23). YouTube shows minimal reach (7 views) with NULL retention, but AutoGluon predicts the highest anxiety-zone score (7.84) — the protocol's efficacy is clear, reach is the gap.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Most listeners felt noticeably better after ten minutes and liked the calming rhythm and music — a solid choice for anxiety relief.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful. Qualitative (n=20) shows split improvement profile with 6/20 each for noticeable and moderate, plus 5 slight. Settle times split between 5 min (9) and 20 min (4). Rhythm rated balanced/comfortable (9), but music opinions split between liked (9) and neutral (5). AutoGluon predicts 7.84, slightly above observed — potential for optimization exists.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Helped some people feel better within five minutes, though opinions on the music were mixed — decent results but not the strongest performer.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful. Qualitative (n=20) shows 8/20 noticeable and 7/20 moderate improvement, with settle time clustering at 10 minutes (10). Rhythm strongly rated as very settling/calming (10), but music reception mixed (8 liked, 5 neutral). AutoGluon prediction (7.84) aligns closely with observed mean.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Provided noticeable improvement for many with anxiety, especially around the ten-minute mark, and the rhythm felt very settling.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful. Crisis protocol shows expected lower mean — structural for acute-distress intake. Qualitative (n=24) reports significant improvement (8) and noticeable (7), with fast settle (5 min for 8). Music liked (11), rhythm balanced/calming (11). AutoGluon predicts 7.96 for crisis zone, the highest — current observed gap may reflect adviser routing opportunity.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Even in acute crisis, people felt significant relief quickly — the protocol works, but few have found it online.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful. Qualitative (n=16) shows moderate improvement (6) and noticeable (5), with settle time at 10 min (5). Rhythm perceived as very settling (8), but music divided between neutral (6) and loved (5). AutoGluon predicts highest crisis efficacy (7.96), matching H-CR-L1 — model strongly validates crisis design.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Calmed many in distress within five to ten minutes; some didn't love the sound, but most found it effective.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, confidence=meaningful. Fast settling is distinctive — 7/19 settle immediately, 5 within 5 minutes. Qualitative shows noticeable (6) and moderate (5) improvement. Rhythm rated balanced (12), but music opinions split with 4 not liking it. Models predict 7.96 — crisis outcomes align with design intent.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Worked fast for those who settled immediately, though a few found the rhythm uncomfortable — results are acceptable but not exceptional.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, confidence=strong. Qualitative (n=15) shows 6/15 noticeable and 5/15 moderate improvement with fast settle (5 min for 5, immediate for 3). Music liked (10), rhythm balanced (7). YouTube views=4 but AutoGluon predicts sleep-zone score 7.54 — strong efficacy signal, minimal reach.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Helped many fall asleep quickly, with most settling within five minutes and enjoying the gentle sound.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, confidence=meaningful. Distinctive listener mix: 6/15 clinician, 5/15 caregiver. Qualitative shows 6/15 noticeable improvement but 2/15 no effect — potential subgroup gap. Settle time longer (10 min for 7). Rhythm too busy (3) for some. Models predict 7.54 but observed slightly lower.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Mixed results — some felt better but a few noticed no effect, and a couple found the rhythm too busy.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful. Qualitative (n=22): 7/22 noticeable, 5/22 moderate, 5/22 significant — strong improvement profile. Settle fast (5 min for 8). Music split (8 liked, 6 neutral), rhythm balanced (9). Views=3, lowest reach, but outcomes align with sleep-zone prediction.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Gave real relief to sleep-seekers, with most settling within five minutes and finding the rhythm calming.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence=meaningful. Qualitative (n=23): 10/23 noticeable improvement, 6/23 significant. Settle at 5 min (7). Rhythm noted as slightly busy (9) — potential engagement friction point. Model predicts highest focus efficacy (7.73) — observed 1.27 points below prediction suggests optimization opportunity.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Helped with focus but some found the rhythm slightly distracting — still, most noticed clearer thinking.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, confidence=meaningful. Qualitative (n=17): 5/17 significant, 5/17 moderate. Settle at 10 min (9) — longer integration. Rhythm balanced (8), music split (6 neutral, 5 loved). Close to AutoGluon prediction (7.73) — solid performer.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Supported mental clarity for many, especially during therapy or caregiving — results were solid if not dramatic.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, confidence=meaningful. Top THRIVING performer with highest dataset mean in series. Qualitative (n=29): 11/29 significant, 7/29 noticeable. Settle at 10 min (15). Music loved (8), rhythm balanced (15). AutoGluon predicts 7.73 — validated. Views=15 modest but higher than peers.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: One of the best for focus — listeners felt sharp and clear, the music was loved, and it's worth trying for concentration.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, confidence=meaningful. Creative flow protocol with slight-to-moderate improvement profile (8/21 slight, 5/21 moderate). Settle times: 6 immediate, 6 at 10 min. Music liked (9), rhythm balanced (9). Not top AutoGluon pick; predicted creative-flow score moderate. Views=2 indicate minimal exposure.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Gave a subtle creative boost — results varied, with some loving it and others feeling only slight change.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, confidence=meaningful. Higher improvement reports than L1: 5/15 significant, 4/15 moderate. Settle at 10 min (5). Music loved (6) and liked (6). AutoGluon not elevated; observed aligns with general creative-flow projection. Views=2.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Helped some feel more creatively focused, with many who loved the music and noticed real improvement.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, confidence=developing. Highest creative-flow mean despite small n. Qualitative (n=25, likely cumulative): 7/25 noticeable, 6/25 significant. Settle at 10 min (7), rhythm balanced (14). Music liked (9). Drift noted at 0.663 but no model elevation — signal vs. analysis divergence.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Strong potential for creative flow — many felt noticeable improvement, though not everyone connected with the music.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, confidence=meaningful. Maintenance protocol with notable settle delay: 9/23 settle at 20 min. Qualitative: 6/23 moderate, 5/23 noticeable. Activity: 8 therapy sessions. Music neutral for 6, rhythm balanced (12). AutoGluon predicts 7.69, slightly above observed. Longer entrainment expected for maintenance.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Helped maintain mental balance over time, though it took longer to feel the effects — good for ongoing regulation.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful. Higher variance than L1. Qualitative (n=16): 5/16 noticeable, 4/16 moderate. Settle faster (5 min for 6). Rhythm split balanced (7) and slightly busy (7). Model predicts 7.69; observed lower with wider spread.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Mixed balance results — some benefited but others found the rhythm busy; outcomes depend on the listener.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, confidence=developing. Small n but high noticeability: 6/19 noticeable, 4/19 moderate. Settle at 5 min (8). Notable: 4/19 did NOT settle — non- responders present. Music neutral (4), rhythm balanced (7). AutoGluon predicts 7.69 — highest maintenance efficacy.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Noticeable benefit for some but four listeners couldn't settle into it — could work with rhythm adjustments.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful. Qualitative (n=22): 7/22 moderate, 6/22 significant — balanced spread. Longer settle: 6/22 at 20 min. Music loved (10). AutoGluon predicts highest cross-zone efficacy (8.23) for low-affect, also cross-bucket to crisis and anxiety — versatile potential but observed lags.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Versatile mood lifter — helped many feel better whether low, anxious, or in crisis, though some took twenty minutes to settle.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful. Qualitative (n=15): 7/15 significant, 4/15 moderate — strong lift. Settle immediate (4) or 20 min (4). Music liked (7). AutoGluon predicts high (8.23) — observed close but below. High drift (0.532).
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Strong mood and energy boost — many felt significant improvement, especially those who could settle in.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing. Lower mean in mood/energy. Qualitative (n=25): 9/25 noticeable, 6/25 significant — decent lift BUT 5/25 no effect. Settle 5 min (8). Music liked (11), rhythm very settling (9). Drift high (0.627). AutoGluon below other X-ME — consistent with lower observed.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Slight improvement for some but five listeners noticed no effect — mixed results overall despite positive music opinions.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful. Motivation protocol shows modest mean despite high improvement reports: 6/17 noticeable, 5/17 significant. Settle fast (5 min for 9). Music liked (8). AutoGluon predicts 7.88 — significant gap suggests routing or entrainment issue.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Boosted motivation for some, working fast for most who tried it, but the mean score is modest.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing. Lowest L2 in series. Qualitative (n=21): 6/21 noticeable, 5/21 moderate. Longer settle: 5/21 at 20 min. Music loved (9), rhythm balanced (9). Highest drift at 0.585 — analytical instability. Model predicts 7.88, gap significant.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Modest effects with a long settle time for many — may need rhythm tweaks to improve impact.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing. HIGH VARIANCE wide distribution despite decent mean. Qualitative (n=17): 8/17 moderate, 5/17 noticeable. Settle split (5 immediate, 5 at 5 min). Music liked (6). Highest analytical drift (0.507) — unstable pattern. AutoGluon does not elevate despite higher mean.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Higher motivation gains but wide variance — some excellent responses alongside weaker ones, indicating instability.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful. Qualitative (n=25): 10/25 noticeable, 8/25 moderate improvement. Settle 5-10 min (8 each). Music loved (9), rhythm balanced (9). AutoGluon predicts X-MC-L2 higher (7.54) — L1 not top pick despite solid outcome. Views=8 decent.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Brought mental clarity for many, especially after five to ten minutes, and most liked the sound.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing. Qualitative (n=20): 7/20 significant, 6/20 moderate — strong improvement. Settle fast: 6 immediate, 6 at 10 min. Rhythm very settling (12), music loved (6). AutoGluon top pick for mind-clarity (7.54). Views=12 highest in TRANSFORMING. Strong candidate despite small n.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: One of the better clarity performers — many felt real lift quickly and loved the calming rhythm.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, confidence=developing. LOWEST performer with HIGHEST variance. Qualitative (n=17): 9/17 noticeable improvement BUT 2/17 it made things worse — the only negative outcome pattern. Rhythm too busy (4) flagged. AutoGluon predicts 7.54 vs observed 5.70 — major design gap. Requires redesign.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Results were mixed at best, with some even feeling worse — the design likely needs adjustment for this population.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The full dataset (n=1000) shows means near 7 across all series, with HEALING demonstrating the tightest variance and TRANSFORMING the broadest spread.
• HEALING protocols (n=574, mean=6.98) lead in consistency, with all three anxiety protocols scoring above 7.
• THRIVING (n=245, mean=6.96) shows peak performance in Focus & Clarity (L3 mean=7.63), validating the HOLD protocol design.
• TRANSFORMING (n=181, mean=6.59) exhibits wider distributions as expected for ascending-arc protocols, with notable variance in Motivation & Drive protocols.
• AutoGluon features identify avg_view_percentage (0.28) and avg_view_duration (0.22) as strongest predictors, suggesting retention engagement is causal, not incidental.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
The three series behave as designed, with HEALING delivering consistent regulation across distress states.
• HEALING descent protocols maintain stable means (6.35-7.22), with crisis protocols showing structurally lower scores consistent with acute intake.
• THRIVING hold protocols achieve the highest peaks in the dataset, particularly T-FC-L3 (mean=7.63), validating cognitive optimization intent.
• TRANSFORMING ascending protocols show mixed outcomes, with X-ME-L1 (mean=6.74) and X-MD-L3 (mean=7.15) diverging sharply, indicating listener subgroup effects in low-affect targeting.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Several protocols deviate from expected patterns in ways that warrant investigation.
• X-MC-L3 (mean=5.70, std=2.45, n=10) is the lowest performer with developing confidence — the negative drift from AutoGluon's predicted 7.54 suggests a design mismatch.
• Cross-bucket routing appears in AutoGluon's predictions: X-ME-L1 is flagged for crisis, anxiety, AND low states — this versatility warrants testing, but current observed scores lag behind predictions.
• Wide cross-run drift (sim<0.70) across 5 Motivation/Mood protocols indicates analytical instability or true population split.
• Low YouTube views (<10) across most HEALING protocols despite strong outcome scores suggest significant undiscovered reach potential.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Targeted protocol development based on convergent analyzer signals.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MC-L3 needs redesign — low observed mean (5.70) vs high predicted (7.54), high variance (2.45), and developing confidence (n=10) indicate the descent curve may lack entrainment for this population.
• T-FC-L3 (n=35, mean=7.63) is the highest THRIVING performer and AutoGluon's top focus prediction (7.73) — recommend primary routing and L2 variant testing for dose-response.
• X-ME-L1 is repeatedly cross-bucket predicted across crisis/anxiety/low zones (score 8.23) but observed mean is 6.74 — expand routing criteria to test broader applicability.
• H-OS-L1 (n=95, views=30) has the highest HEALING reach but retention data is NULL — prioritize YouTube analytics to confirm whether high views translate to entrainment completion.
• Several analyzers flagged X-MD-L3 instability (high drift 0.507, variance 2.38) despite decent mean — monitor before scaling, investigate population split.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding analysis reveals widespread analytical drift and insufficient data for semantic clustering.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: 29 of 30 protocols show similarity <0.85, with X-MD-L3 (0.507), X-ME-L2 (0.532), and X-MD-L2 (0.585) showing notable instability — this indicates either evolving response patterns or model sensitivity.
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Insufficient embedding history for clustering — too few protocols with multi-run embeddings.
• Confidence note: Drift signals are reliable indicators of analytical change but lack clustering context for definitive interpretation. The prevalence of high drift across TRANSFORMING protocols (9 of 9) is particularly notable.

by_protocol.X-MD-L3: n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing. HIGH VARIANCE wide distribution despite decent mean. Qualitative (n=17): 8/17 moderate, 5/17 noticeable. Settle split (5 immediate, 5 at 5 min). Music liked (6). Highest analytical drift (0.507) — unstable pattern. AutoGluon does not elevate despite higher mean.
by_protocol.X-ME-L1: n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful. Qualitative (n=22): 7/22 moderate, 6/22 significant — balanced spread. Longer settle: 6/22 at 20 min. Music loved (10). AutoGluon predicts highest cross-zone efficacy (8.23) for low-affect, also cross-bucket to crisis and anxiety — versatile potential but observed lags.
by_protocol.X-ME-L2: n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful. Qualitative (n=15): 7/15 significant, 4/15 moderate — strong lift. Settle immediate (4) or 20 min (4). Music liked (7). AutoGluon predicts high (8.23) — observed close but below. High drift (0.532).
by_protocol.X-ME-L3: n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing. Lower mean in mood/energy. Qualitative (n=25): 9/25 noticeable, 6/25 significant — decent lift BUT 5/25 no effect. Settle 5 min (8). Music liked (11), rhythm very settling (9). Drift high (0.627). AutoGluon below other X-ME — consistent with lower observed.

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: Most listeners felt noticeably better after ten minutes and liked the calming rhythm and music — a solid choice for anxiety relief.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: Helped some people feel better within five minutes, though opinions on the music were mixed — decent results but not the strongest performer.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Provided noticeable improvement for many with anxiety, especially around the ten-minute mark, and the rhythm felt very settling.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: Even in acute crisis, people felt significant relief quickly — the protocol works, but few have found it online.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: Calmed many in distress within five to ten minutes; some didn't love the sound, but most found it effective.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Worked fast for those who settled immediately, though a few found the rhythm uncomfortable — results are acceptable but not exceptional.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: One of the most helpful protocols for overstimulation — most people felt better within ten minutes and loved the music, though YouTube reach is still small.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Reduced overstimulation for many after about ten minutes, with very low YouTube exposure despite consistent benefits.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Calmed people struggling with overwhelm, especially those using it for sleep; improvement was common though not universal.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Helped many fall asleep quickly, with most settling within five minutes and enjoying the gentle sound.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Mixed results — some felt better but a few noticed no effect, and a couple found the rhythm too busy.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Gave real relief to sleep-seekers, with most settling within five minutes and finding the rhythm calming.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Gave a subtle creative boost — results varied, with some loving it and others feeling only slight change.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Helped some feel more creatively focused, with many who loved the music and noticed real improvement.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Strong potential for creative flow — many felt noticeable improvement, though not everyone connected with the music.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Helped with focus but some found the rhythm slightly distracting — still, most noticed clearer thinking.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Supported mental clarity for many, especially during therapy or caregiving — results were solid if not dramatic.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: One of the best for focus — listeners felt sharp and clear, the music was loved, and it's worth trying for concentration.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Helped maintain mental balance over time, though it took longer to feel the effects — good for ongoing regulation.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Mixed balance results — some benefited but others found the rhythm busy; outcomes depend on the listener.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Noticeable benefit for some but four listeners couldn't settle into it — could work with rhythm adjustments.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Brought mental clarity for many, especially after five to ten minutes, and most liked the sound.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: One of the better clarity performers — many felt real lift quickly and loved the calming rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Results were mixed at best, with some even feeling worse — the design likely needs adjustment for this population.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Boosted motivation for some, working fast for most who tried it, but the mean score is modest.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Modest effects with a long settle time for many — may need rhythm tweaks to improve impact.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Higher motivation gains but wide variance — some excellent responses alongside weaker ones, indicating instability.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Versatile mood lifter — helped many feel better whether low, anxious, or in crisis, though some took twenty minutes to settle.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Strong mood and energy boost — many felt significant improvement, especially those who could settle in.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Slight improvement for some but five listeners noticed no effect — mixed results overall despite positive music opinions.
